        - 明确区分“事实信息”“原作者观点”“需要验证的问题”。
        - 投资相关内容只做研究观察，不给买卖建议、仓位建议或交易指令。
        - 优先提取对 AI 基建、半导体/PCB/光通信、工具链、市场情绪、个人效率有用的信号。
        - 对低信噪比、重复、鸡汤、缺少来源的内容要直接降权。
        - 输出要适合 Telegram 阅读：重点清晰，避免长篇复述。

        ### 1. $GLW erased $52B in market value in 4 days. That same company just took a $3.2B investment from Nvidia, signed 3 hyperscaler deals worth up to $6B each, and grew its AI segment ...

                $GLW erased $52B in market value in 4 days.

That same company just took a $3.2B investment from Nvidia, signed 3 hyperscaler deals worth up to $6B each, and grew its AI segment 36% last quarter.

Someone is wrong. Let me show you who.

1. What actually happened

Corning ran 256% over 12 months. Up 105% in 2026 alone. Then it dropped 24% in 4 days on zero company news.

No earnings miss. No guidance cut. No contract loss. It was pure AI de risking, the same rotation that hit every semi and AI infrastructure name that week.

When a stock falls 24% and the analysts respond by RAISING targets, pay attention:

BofA: $223 to $243, Buy reiterated.
Oppenheimer: $210 to $230, Outperform.
UBS: modeling ~$30B revenue by 2028 and $40B by 2030. Corning did $14.5B in 2024. That's nearly a 3x.
2. The business everyone can see: fiber
Q1 2026 numbers:

Total revenue: $4.35B, up 18% YoY. Beat.
EPS up 30% YoY.
Optical Communications: $1.85B, up 36% YoY, against $1.7B consensus.
Optical segment net income: $387M, up 93% YoY. Income growing 2.6x faster than revenue. That's operating leverage, not a demand blip.
Solar: $370M, up 80%.

Why it's durable and not one quarter of hype:

$META signed a multiyear deal worth up to $6B for fiber. Then Corning announced two MORE hyperscaler agreements, each similar in size and duration. That's up to ~$18B of contracted demand from 3 customers.

Then $NVDA committed up to $3.2B and is building 3 new US fiber factories with them (North Carolina and Texas). Nvidia doesn't invest billions into suppliers. It did it for Corning.

The reason: an AI data center needs roughly 10x the fiber of a traditional one, and networking is shifting toward optics (CPO) as copper hits its bandwidth wall.

3. The business almost nobody is pricing: glass substrates

### 2. 大家以為玻璃基板是在換材料。 等一下，不是。 它是在換掉 AI 封裝的成本函數。 Reticle limit 約 26×33mm，限制的是單次曝光，不是整個 package。台積電可以靠 stitching，把 CoWoS 從 3.5×、5.5×一路往 9.5×甚至更大推。 所以 CoWoS 不是做不下去。 問題是越做越貴。 當 GPU、HBM、I/O...
                - Capture ID: ai-x:2076399935465902336
                - 时间: 2026-07-13 05:00
                - 来源: x.com / post
                - 作者: Leoskie_L
                - 标签: ai-x-discovery, hbm_supply, emerging_term, Advanced Packaging
                - 优先级: urgent
                - 去重状态: unique
                - URL: https://x.com/Leoskie_L/status/2076399935465902336
                - 本地路径: inbox/browser-capture/2026-07-13/aix_2076399935465902336.md

                大家以為玻璃基板是在換材料。

等一下，不是。

它是在換掉 AI 封裝的成本函數。

Reticle limit 約 26×33mm，限制的是單次曝光，不是整個 package。台積電可以靠 stitching，把 CoWoS 從 3.5×、5.5×一路往 9.5×甚至更大推。

所以 CoWoS 不是做不下去。

問題是越做越貴。

當 GPU、HBM、I/O、RDL、電源全部塞進同一個 package，每多一塊，對位、翹曲、良率和晶圓利用率就一起惡化。

這時候，方形 panel 才開始有意義。

300mm wafer 面積約 70,686mm²；515×510mm panel 約 262,650mm²，是 3.72 倍。更重要的是，AI package 本身也是方形。封裝越大，圓形 wafer 邊緣浪費越誇張。

所以 CoPoS 的核心不是「玻璃比較先進」。

是方形 panel 對大型方形 package 的經濟學，可能比圓形 wafer 更合理。

但 CoPoS 也不是整張玻璃塞滿 GPU、不切直接出貨。

更可能的路線，是用 panel 完成大型 RDL、TGV、build-up 和封裝整合，最後再切成個別 package。

Panel 是製程載體，不一定是最後產品。

玻璃的優點是真的：低 CTE、高剛性、尺寸穩定，適合大尺寸 fine-line RDL。

但每個優點後面都有代價。

玻璃不容易翹，但會裂；表面平滑，但銅和 SiO₂ 不好黏；導熱比樹脂好，但絕對取代不了冷板和液冷。

所以玻璃不是解決問題。

它只是把 organic core 的翹曲問題，換成 TGV、附著、鍍銅、切割、搬運和 microcrack 問題。

而且 Glass core 不代表 ABF 消失。

真正的結構更可能是：

ABF build-up
＋ Glass core
＋ TGV
＋ Copper interconnect
＋ Fine-line RDL

玻璃取代的是中間 core，不是整個 substrate。味之素、欣興、南電、景碩未必直接出局，真正發生的是價值重新分配。

所以台股真正有機會的，也不是誰家倉庫裡有玻璃。

而是誰能讓一張大 panel 經過十幾道製程後，還是沒有裂、沒有翹、沒有 open、沒有 void，而且每一張都
